revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従八位]][[ko:종8위]][[zh:從八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従八位]][[ko:종8위]][[zh:從八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従八位]][[ko:종8위]][[zh:從八位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:従八位]][[ko:종8위]][[zh:從八位]]
{{No footnotes|date=2015th year 8月}}
'''Jūhachi-i''' (従八位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Senior Eighth Rank|ja|正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Senior Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Senior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位|1=Senior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
In the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, Jūhachi-i was further divided into two subRanks: Upper Jūhachi-i (従八位上) and Lower Jūhachi-i (従八位下). It corresponded to positions such as minor officials in the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|qid=Q6867690|lt=Ministry of the Center|1=Ministry of the Center}}, {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|en|Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)|qid=Q10829855|lt=Ministry of Ceremonies|1=Ministry of Ceremonies (Japan)}} (治部少解部), and {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|qid=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}} (刑部少解部).
In the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125768223}}, these subRanks were eliminated. Jūhachi-i corresponded to positions such as scribes in the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} and assistants in the {{ill|Daijōkan|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125768223}}.
Under the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), Jūhachi-i became the lowest Rank in the hierarchy.
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/e-Gov Law Search|qq=}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive
{{Court Ranks of Japan}} *
{{translated page|ja|従八位|version=103189894|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:従八位]]

Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=}} Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位|1=Senior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Ninth Rank|qq=}} Ranks of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
